title: fix: Fix parameter mismatch in EPUBBookLoaderHelper.translate_with_backoff
body:
Problem: The translate_with_backoff method in EPUBBookLoaderHelper was raising a TypeError due to a parameter mismatch between its signature and how it is called through the backoff decorator. The error observed was:
TypeError: EPUBBookLoaderHelper.translate_with_backoff() takes 1 positional argument but 3 were given

Solution (summary):
1) Change the method signature to explicitly accept parameters (text and context_flag) and still pass through any additional kwargs to the underlying translator.
   - New signature: translate_with_backoff(self, text, context_flag=False, **kwargs)
   - Forwarding: return self.translate_model.translate(text=text, context_flag=context_flag, **kwargs)
2) Add jitter=None to the backoff decorator to align with the library’s usage and avoid unintended jitter behavior.
3) Improve error handling and logging around translation attempts to aid debugging and observability.

Notes:
- Existing callers that used positional args (e.g., self.translate_with_backoff(p.text, self.context_flag)) will continue to work with the new positional mapping (text -> p.text, context_flag -> self.context_flag).
- The updated signature also supports explicit keyword usage if desired in future refactors.
- This change minimizes surface area while fixing the mismatch and improving resilience via backoff customization and better logging.

step_plan.summary: This PR resolves a parameter mismatch in EPUBBookLoaderHelper.translate_with_backoff by updating its signature to explicitly accept text and context_flag, forwarding additional kwargs to the translator, configuring the backoff decorator with jitter=None, and enhancing error handling and logging for better observability.

steps.operation: Refactor translate_with_backoff to explicit signature (text, context_flag=False, **kwargs) and forward kwargs to translate_model.translate; set backoff decorator jitter=None; enhance error handling and logging
steps.reason: Fix the TypeError caused by a parameter mismatch when the method is wrapped by a backoff decorator. The new explicit signature (text, context_flag) preserves backward compatibility with existing positional calls (text -> p.text, context_flag -> self.context_flag) while enabling future keyword usage. Adding jitter=None aligns with the library's usage and prevents unintended jitter. Enhanced error handling/logging improves observability during translation retries.
steps.side_effects: Backward compatibility for existing positional call sites is preserved; tests and mocks referencing the old signature may remain valid but could require updates to reflect the explicit parameters; backoff jitter behavior will become deterministic (jitter=None), which may affect retry timing; improved logging will surface in translation retry logs and aid debugging; no changes to core translation logic.
